rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE AUDREY G. WILLIAMS REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #5, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/30/2022. The following issues must be addressed: 1) Paragraph 8 of the petition: The attorney's declaration re the paralegals' compliance with section 6450 of the Business and Professions Code must be provided. 2) Paragraph 9: The bond was increased to $600,000 by the 09/29/2022 order, but no additional bond has been filed. 3) Paragraph 10: The addresses of Kiah Jefferies and Joel Jefferies are different from those listed on the proof of service. Their current addresses must be confirmed by a verified allegation. 4) Schedules E & F: Please provide documentation supporting the stated market value of the real property as of 06/30/2022. 5) Schedule H: Did the new tenant pay a security deposit? 6) Exhibit A: The $200 filing fee for the 4th account was already paid.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TEDMAN MARK
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/20/2022. The following issues must be addressed: 1) No proposed order has been received. 2) No courtesy copy of the petition has been received. 3) Notice must be given on the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-120, with a copy of the most recent remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6). 4) A specific period during which administration of the estate is sought to be continued must be proposed. This issue must be addressed by an amendment or amended petition with new notice. 5) The status report must include the following allegations to show the condition of the estate: i) notice to all known or reasonably ascertainable creditors; ii) creditor claims filed or presented, including claims by public entities under Probate Code sections 9201 and 9202; iii) outstanding debts and liabilities of the estate, including taxes; iv) assets on hand; and v) assignees of beneficiaries or other third parties such as power of attorney agents to whom distribution may be made, if any.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TEDMAN MARK

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALICE M. WHITE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/13/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The math in the Schedule of Distribution is incorrect. An amendment must be filed and noticed. The administrator is holding funds in a bank in Washington, not in this state. [Probate Code Section 9700.] Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary has previously been confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. Please file a copy of the seller's closing statement. The Summary of Account shown in paragraph 10 of the petition is the correct form; the Summary that precedes the schedules is not quite correct. Gains on sale are not "asset changes" that merely back out the cash on hand from the net sale proceeds. This estate's interest in the real property at the time of sale should have been shown on a gains schedule with the appraised value subtracted from it to arrive at the total gain on sale. Costs associated with the sale should have been shown on the disbursements schedule. The accounting does not reflect payment of the Probate Referee's commission. If she has not yet been paid, she must receive notice. Is the administrator not seeking reimbursement of the $50 she used to open the estate account? Courtesy copies of the amendment and proof of service (and a revised proposed order) must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 12/2/22. If the amendment cannot be filed in time to serve notice of it before the 12/13/22 hearing, Counsel should contact the assigned examiner to request a further continuance. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Ordinarily, detailed schedules of receipts and gains and losses on sale must be filed to support the statutory fee. [CRC 7.705(b) and 7.550(b)(6).] In light of the history of this case and reported lack of cash on the date of death, those additional schedules will not be required for this case. Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] There were actually two I&As filed. It appears that the one filed on 4/26/22 (not 4/26/21) should have been a CORRECTED Final I&A.)

rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF IVY HUANG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/13/2022. 1) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the GC-020 form. 2) The second amended petition superseded the first amended petition as well as the initial petition. The beginning date of the second amended account must be 8/31/21, as the superseded accounting schedules cannot be incorporated by reference. Either an amendment to the second amended petition or a third amended petition must be filed to add the accounting from 8/31/21.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ALBERT SAMARIS SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST, DATED OCTOBER 15, 2004
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION RE SUBSTITUTED JUDGMENT TRUST
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/20/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: An amended petition was filed on 10/13/22. Due to a shortage of court clerks, it was not actually e-filed until 10/17/22, when it was available for viewing. Mailing notice before the actual filing (e-filing) of the pleading requiring notice is defective. (The proof of service indicates, in any event, that notice was mailed on 10/11/22, before the amended petition was even uploaded for e-filing, and the Notice of Hearing did not reflect that an AMENDED petition had been filed.) The amended petition was not signed and verified by the trustee. [Probate Code Sect. 1023 and CRC 7.103(c).] The amended petition is also incomplete: no exhibits are attached. A declaration by the trustee or attorney who retains the original statements must be filed. [CRC 7.575(b) and LRSF 14.34 D.] Only the statements verifying the ending balances are required for interim accountings. Why are the statements in the name of Louis Samaris, not as trustee of the SNT? A complete second amended petition, signed and verified by the trustee, must be filed. After it is e-filed, notice must be given of the amended petition. If the complete second amended petition cannot be filed in time to give 30 days' notice for the 12/20/22 hearing, Counsel should contact the assigned examiner to request a further continuance. Courtesy copies of second amended petition and proof of service must be received by the assigned examiner by no later than 12/9/22. (There is no LRSF 14.91.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE 2007 MILTON J. MOSK REVOCABLE TRUST DATED AUGUST 28, 2007
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Asset
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/20/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: In a supplement to the petition, signed by the co-trustees and verified by at least one of them, please address the following issues: (1) A beneficiary of the trust receives mailed notice with a copy of the petition, but any heir-at-law, who would stand to inherit, if the trust did not exist or were invalid, is a party whose right, title or interest would be affected by this petition, and, therefore, must receive (or waive) personal service of notice. [Prob. C. Sect. 17203(b) and CCP Sect. 413.10 et seq.] Are all of the parties who were listed in the petition and received notice ALL of the heirs-at-law of the trustor? (2) Why was the Pacific Life Insurance Company correspondence addressed to Gerry Salberg? (3) Did Melvin B. Mosk decline to serve? (How did Fay Goodman become a co-trustee?) (4) The trust is actually the 2007 Milton J. Mosk Revocable Trust, as amended 9/2/20. Should the order not make this clear? Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 12/9/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SACHIKO KJERBO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/14/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: A Proof of Subscribing Witness, in addition to not being required for a self-proving will, is not sufficient evidence to overcome the presumption of revocation. The attorney's declaration that she drafted the estate planning documents and that, to her knowledge, the decedent did not revoke her will or make additional changes to her trust, is not evidence that the testator did not destroy the original will in her possession. [Probate Code Section 6124.] Were the predeceased siblings Yukio Lee and Tadashi Lee not survived by issue? Is the nephew Hideki Yamamura the only issue of Syoko Lee? If there are additional nieces or nephews by predeceased siblings, whether or not they are trust beneficiaries, they must be listed and must receive notice. (The notice service list includes deceased people.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 12/5/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
